Plastics Technologies, Additive Manufacturing, Materials Science of Metals, 3D Metal Printing, Manufacturing Technology, Materials Science of Plastics, Powder Technology, Structure Optimisation, Design Rules, Product Creation, Standard Software Application Development

program_name: Advanced and Computational Mathematics – International Master's Programme

accepted_tests.test_name: Cambridge Preliminary English Test + Result Distinction (PET)

english_requirements.notes: Completed degree in English Studies or American Studies; Completed degree with English as the language of instruction; Proof of professional qualification as a state-examined interpreter/translator; Applicants from countries with English as official/educational or native language are not required to submit proof of English language proficiency.

german_requirements.notes: Basic knowledge of German is recommended but not compulsory; Courses to obtain the required level of German language (A2) are included within the curriculum for non-native speakers.

course_content_summary: Advanced and Computational Mathematics Master's programme focuses on solving real-world problems, advanced mathematics, computational mathematics, and data sciences. Students gain expertise in Advanced Pure Mathematics, Computational Mathematics, or Data Sciences. The curriculum includes a seminar, Master's thesis, and mandatory German language courses. The program prepares students for careers in scientific research, academia, and innovation in tech-driven fields. Outstanding achievers may qualify for a PhD.

program_name: Advanced Architecture — From Urban Design to Building Construction

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: 30 ECTS can be chosen from the German Master’s degree programme in “Design and Construction“ (“Entwurf und Konstruktion”)

modules.condition_comment: Chosen from the German Master’s degree programme in “Design and Construction“ (“Entwurf und Konstruktion”)

english_requirements.notes: Native English speakers as well as graduates from English-language Bachelor's programmes are exempt.

course_content_summary: Advanced Architecture program focusing on design and construction across all scales, from urban contexts to single details. Key topics include urban design, design of buildings in existing contexts, built environments, and new building design. The curriculum integrates internationally-oriented projects, elective modules (including 30 ECTS from the German Master’s degree programme in “Design and Construction”), and Master’s theses. Students develop skills to translate abstract designs into concrete implementations, considering professional, practical, technical, economic, ecological, social, cultural, historical, and climatic aspects. The program emphasizes execution planning and scientific research in design and construction. Graduates are prepared for roles in architectural, engineering, and construction companies, the construction industry, property development, housing associations, real estate, public sector, and R&D in design and construction.

program_name: Advanced Computational Methods in Civil Engineering

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: at least 20 CP each must be demonstrated from two of the areas listed below

accepted_tests.test_name: RWTH Aachen Language Center Placement Test

english_requirements.notes: Placement test of the RWTH Aachen Language Center can only be obtained by individuals who are already enrolled as students at RWTH Aachen.

course_content_summary: Advanced Computational Methods in Civil Engineering (AdCom) focuses on applying and developing numerical methods and simulations for innovative civil engineering structures. The interdisciplinary curriculum includes courses from Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Mathematics, covering structural mechanics, fluid mechanics, and numerical mathematics. Students learn to master challenges with new resource-conserving materials, advanced composite materials, and lightweight structures subject to dynamic loads. Expertise includes numerical methods, mechanical principles, software code development, and assessing numerical modelling limits. The program is highly scientific and research-oriented, preparing graduates for PhD programs and scientific careers in research or industry, with a strong focus on research and international reputation.

english_requirements.notes: Completed degree in English Studies or American Studies; Completed degree with English as the language of instruction; Proof of professional qualification as a state-examined interpreter/translator; Applicants from countries with English as official/educational or native language are not required to submit proof of English language proficiency within their application.

german_requirements.notes: Courses to obtain the required level of German language (A2 by the end of the third semester) are included within the curriculum for non-native speakers. Students providing a certificate of A2 level (CEFR) may choose advanced language courses or subject studies.

course_content_summary: Advanced chemical and physical aspects of modern materials, synthesis, development, analysis, manufacturing of functional materials. Interdisciplinary program based on materials chemistry and materials physics. Research-oriented extension and intensification of physical and chemical knowledge, advanced courses in modern research fields, lab-oriented modules, research project, Master's thesis.

university_name: Rosenheim Technical University of Applied Sciences

english_requirements.notes: Native English-speaking applicants are not required to submit proof. Accepted: At least six years of English tuition at school with a grade of at least “sufficient” in the final year proven by a German-language university entrance qualification or an equivalent, recognised university entrance qualification from a non-German school. Completed English-language Bachelor's or Master's degree. Completed English studies university degree in Germany or abroad. Passing "Technical English", "Business English" or a comparable English module with a grade of 3.0 or better worth at least 4 ECTS from a German university degree.

course_content_summary: Advanced Industrial Engineering, business administration, sustainable concepts, digital technologies, artificial intelligence, data science, collaborative robots, augmented reality, virtual reality, mixed reality, engineering practice, production engineer, technical buyer, technical sales, supply chain management, management consultant, international requirements, technical English skills.

english_requirements.notes: Completed degree in English Studies or American Studies; Completed degree with English as the language of instruction; Proof of professional qualification as a state-examined interpreter/translator; Applicants from countries with English as official/educational or native language are not required to submit proof of English language proficiency within their application.

german_requirements.notes: From winter semester 2024/25 German language proficiency A1 is compulsory! Applicants applying for a degree programme offered in English language need to show proof of German language proficiency at a minimum of an A1 level at the time of enrolment. Until the end of their third study semester at Chemnitz University of Technology, they have to submit a German language proof at level A2. German language courses at level A2 will be offered at our university. German language attendance certificates will not be accepted. It is also possible to take an online course before the semester starts (440 EUR fee, details in the admission letter).

course_content_summary: Advanced Manufacturing, Mechanical Engineering, Production, cutting-edge scientific methods, innovative technologies, advanced manufacturing processes, modern manufacturing, special mathematical methods of engineering, Fourier transforms, regression calculation, probability, mathematical statistics, scientific and engineering data processing, CAD, CAS, numerical simulation, data acquisition, multiphysics simulation, practical experience, metrology, control engineering, sensors, actuators, digital methods of manufacturing, new materials for engineering, polymers, metals, composites, matrix systems, functional properties, in-depth theoretical basics of engineering, engineering mechanics, design, manufacturing, fluid dynamics, resource-efficient manufacturing concepts, technical and natural cycles and networks, system optimisation, energy concepts, soft skills, responsible decisions, political developments, geographical constellations, regional economics, research, teaching, natural sciences, human factors, economics, integrated internships, industry, business, Hybrid Technologies, Printed Functionalities, Work Design, Sustainability Management, Production Systems, transdisciplinary, faculty-overarching, future-oriented, intercultural competencies.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of obtaining a local higher education entrance qualification in one of the following countries: Australia, Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, USA, United Kingdom, South Africa or: Proof of a Bachelor’s degree in English in one of the following countries: Australia, Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, USA, United Kingdom, South Africa. Other language certificates may be accepted following a case-by-case assessment.

course_content_summary: The Master's degree program in Advanced Materials at Justus Liebig University Giessen (JLU) focuses on modern functional materials modified at the molecular level for specific applications. It addresses questions related to extending electric car battery life, increasing solar cell efficiency, and developing biocompatible implants. The program integrates interdisciplinary knowledge from chemistry and physics to understand and optimize material properties. It is research-oriented, providing theoretical and practical knowledge to tailor materials for future applications, with a strong integration into current materials research in Giessen, focusing on high-performance and energy materials. Students can work on current research projects and choose a specialization. The program offers excellent career opportunities and the unique opportunity to obtain a double degree with partner universities like Osaka University (Japan), Kansai University (Japan), and the University of Padua (Italy).

required_degree_domains: Engineering Science with a major in Materials Science
Natural Science with a major in Physics
Natural Science with a major in Chemistry
course_content_summary: Advanced Materials Analysis (AMA) Master's program for materials science, engineering science, physics, or chemistry graduates. Focus on behavior and analysis of solid materials down to atomic level, combining natural scientist's and engineer's perspectives. Utilizes various instrumental analysis techniques for materials characterization, structure-property relations, and targeted tailoring. Covers analysis of advanced steels, electronics materials, shape memory alloys, and energy materials using microscopic, spectroscopic, and diffraction methods. Prepares graduates for careers in materials-related industrial fields, research and development, metallurgy, and semiconductor industry, as well as academic research.
